macro_segments.title: Introduction and the Mitzvah of Shiluach Haken: Active vs. Passive Obligation

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and framing of the daf; discussion of yesterday's context on hekdesh (sanctified bird)
Baraita on 'ki yikarei' (when it chances upon you): not an obligation to seek out the mitzvah
Baraita's application of 'kan mikol makom' (nest in all cases) and 'lefanecha' (before you) to include any type of nest location

macro_segments.title: The Meaning of 'Lefanecha' and 'Baderech': Control vs. Location

micro_segments.title: Analysis of 'lefanecha' as referring to property under your control; cases of pigeons in dovecotes, attics, gardens
Distinction between birds in your domain but not under control (obligatory shiluach) vs. birds in captivity (exempt)
Gemara's rejection of baraita's derasha; reversion to 'ki yikarei' and 'lefanecha' as the primary limitations

macro_segments.title: Expansion of 'Baderech': Ocean, Sky, and Person's Head

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's teaching: bird found in ocean qualifies via 'baderech' (path/way)
Discussion of birds in sky (eagle/vulture); distinction between 'derech nesher' and 'derech' without qualification
Papunaim ask Rav Masna: nest on a person's head; answer involving adama (ground-connected) status

macro_segments.title: Digression: Hints of Biblical Figures (Moshe, Haman, Esther, Mordechai) and the Maharsha's Analysis

micro_segments.title: Series of questions on deriving names Moshe, Haman, Esther, Mordechai from Torah verses and gematria
Maharsha's interpretation: these non-Hebrew names are validated as fitting through Torah allusions
Discussion of why non-Hebrew (Egyptian, Persian) names and the Ibn Ezra's claim about Megillah authorship

macro_segments.title: Herodian Doves: Fantastic Story and the Question of Non-Kosher Bird Control

micro_segments.title: Rav Kahana's account of Herodian doves in vast formations; the rebellious bird and strange dialogue
Rav Ashi's dismissal and Gemara's clarification: fantastical elements and magical interpretation

macro_segments.title: Herodian Doves: Fantastic Story and the Question of Non-Kosher Bird Control (continued)

micro_segments.title: Connection to mezuman (controlled birds): Herodian doves as example of fully captured birds exempt from shiluach

macro_segments.title: Non-Kosher Birds (Of Tamei): The Extended Debate on Word 'Tzippor'

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yitzchak's position: word 'tzippor' (bird) excludes non-kosher species; proof from 'kan tzippor' phrasing
Series of challenges to the 'tzippor = kosher only' rule using verses from prohibitions on images, praise of animals, etc.
Gemara's sustained defense: 'tzippor' means kosher; non-kosher indicated by broader term 'kanaf' (winged)

macro_segments.title: The Case of 'Kol Tzippor Tehora Tocheilu': Non-Kosher Species vs. Prohibited Kosher Birds

micro_segments.title: Inference from 'eat all kosher birds': does it imply non-kosher tzipporim exist, or only kosher species?
Gemara's answer: 'tehora' excludes specific prohibited kosher birds (treifa, shechuta d'metzora), not non-kosher species
Positive mitzvah (asei) and negative mitzvah (lo ta'asei): redundancy to highlight special case of metzora bird

macro_segments.title: Two Live Birds in Metzora Purification: 'Chayot' and 'Tehorot' Examined

micro_segments.title: Analysis of 'shtei tzipporim chayot' in metzora purification; meaning of 'chayot' (alive/living)
Distinction: 'chayot' indicates whole limbs, not necessarily kosher vs. non-kosher; 'tehorot' (pure/kosher) is separate
Rebbe Yishmael's principle: machshir u'mechaper (purifying and atoning) have same requirements as korban

macro_segments.title: Excluded Cases for Metzora Birds: Treifa, Ir Hanidachat, Defiled Offerings

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's case: birds bought with proceeds of idolatry; 'charem kamohu' (forbidden in benefit)
Ravina's case: bird that killed a person (shor haniskal); forbidden in benefit, exempt from shiluach
Summary: word 'tzippor' = kosher species only; 'tehorot' = excludes various disqualified birds via derasha

macro_segments.title: Eggs vs. Mother Bird: Separate Obligations and Treifa Status

micro_segments.title: Non-kosher mother on kosher eggs: exempt (no tzippor); non-kosher eggs with kosher mother: obligated (mother is tzippor)
Baraita: treifa mother = obligated in shiluach; treifa chicks = exempt; each has separate rule
Rav Kahana's source: 'tikach lach' (take for yourself) = chicks must be edible; if treifa, not edible, so exempt

macro_segments.title: Edge Case: Half-Slaughtered Chick and Status Changes

micro_segments.title: Rav Hoshaia's query: if I partially slaughter a chick (making it treifa), can I now grab the mother?
Two positions: either treifa status exempts now, or potential to complete makes it still obligatory
Gemara leaves as 'teiku' (unresolved); intuitive answer likely favors obligation

macro_segments.title: Complex Nest Scenarios: Division, Fertilization, Stacking, and Cross-Species Brooding

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yirmiya's questions: cloth/feathers between mother and eggs, unfertilized eggs, two-level nests
Male on female, female on male, father on eggs: unclear if division/obstruction prevents obligation
All of Yirmiya's questions left as 'teiku' (unresolved)
Rabbi Zeira's question: dove on tasiya eggs or reverse; Abaye's inference from baraita about mixed species
Baraita: non-kosher mother on kosher eggs or vice versa = exempt; implies kosher mother on kosher eggs (even if not hers) = obligated
Gemara: possible exception for korei (male partridge) where Torah explicitly mentions brooding non-own eggs

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Korei Zachar (Male Partridge) — Rabbi Eliezer vs. Chachamim Dispute

micro_segments.title: Mishnah text: 'Korei zachar' (male partridge) — Rabbi Eliezer obligates, Chachamim exempt
Rabbi Abahu's explanation: verb 'gara' (to brood) in Jeremiah and Psalms applies to non-own eggs; Rabbi Eliezer uses this as source
Gemara: mishnah specifies 'male' and 'korei' to show both issues present; females agreed upon by all; other males exempt
Final clarification: only male partridge is disputed; normal male birds exempt; female partridge all agree obligated

topical_tags.term: Ki Yikarei (if it chances upon you)
Shechuta d'metzora (bird slaughtered for leper purification)
Machshir u'mechaper (purifying and atoning outside Temple)
Shor ha'niskal (animal that killed a person)
Ir ha'nidachat (city that turned to idolatry)
